## 2026-06-18T08:36:14.867Z — Project identity
- kind: summary
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_908505068e0d92bb"}

TekBreed OSS (@tekbreed/oss) is an open-source monorepo for AI infrastructure, hosted at github.com/tekbreed/tekbreed-oss. The founder and lead maintainer is Christopher Sesugh (github.com/sponsors/christophersesugh). Licensed under MIT. Docs site at oss.tekbreed.com.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:14.946Z — First product family: TekMemo
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_451bec9152525290"}

TekMemo is the first product family of TekBreed OSS. It is a file-first memory runtime for AI apps, agents, coding tools, and MCP clients. Published as three npm packages: @tekbreed/tekmemo (core runtime), @tekbreed/tekmemo-cli (CLI), and @tekbreed/tekmemo-mcp-server (MCP server).

## 2026-06-18T08:36:15.401Z — Monorepo toolchain
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_78f929e6e3ea3f69"}

Built with pnpm (v9.15.4) workspaces, Turborepo (v2.9.16) for task orchestration, Biome (v2.4.16) for formatting/linting, tsdown (v0.22.2) for package bundling, TypeScript 6.0.3, Vitest (v4.1.8) for testing, and Changesets for versioning/changelog. Requires Node.js >=22.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:16.842Z — Monorepo structure
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_4f83feaef3dc5155"}

Monorepo directory layout: apps/docs (VitePress docs site), apps/tekmemo-mcp-worker (Cloudflare Worker), packages/tekmemo (core runtime), packages/tekmemo-cli (CLI), packages/tekmemo-mcp-server (MCP server), packages/tekmemo-adapter-openai, packages/tekmemo-adapter-upstash, packages/tekmemo-adapter-voyage, packages/tekmemo-benchmark-kit, packages/tekmemo-testing (test utilities), benchmarks/, tooling/ (private @repo/* packages: tsdown-config, typescript-config, utils), docs/ (repo operations), scripts/ (maintenance), assets/ (images).

## 2026-06-18T08:36:18.208Z — Core package structure (tekmemo)
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_8485263fc0950f96"}

@tekbreed/tekmemo core package exports from a single root entrypoint. Internal modules: agentfs/, ai-sdk/, cloud-client/, core/, fs/, graph/, recall/, rerank/, tekmemo/, testing/. All public APIs are re-exported from src/index.ts. Peer dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (optional), ai (optional). Runtime dependency: zod. Exposes dual ESM/CJS builds via tsdown.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:19.188Z — Canonical memory protocol
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_17c11233c0dd3f52"}

The canonical TekMemo local memory layout uses a .tekmemo/ directory with: manifest.json, memory/core.md, memory/notes.md, events/memory-events.jsonl, events/conversations.jsonl, indexes/chunks.jsonl, graph/nodes.jsonl, graph/edges.jsonl, snapshots/snapshots.jsonl. The core protocol contracts are provider-neutral and owned by @tekbreed/tekmemo.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:20.359Z — Architecture boundary rules
- kind: constraint
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_80a532bef6fb4840"}

Architecture boundaries: Core protocol contracts must stay provider-neutral (no filesystem, API calls, or cloud infra). NodeFs storage owns filesystem persistence. Recall/vector contracts are provider-neutral with adapter patterns. Reranking contracts are provider-neutral. Provider adapters (OpenAI, VoyageAI, Upstash) translate contracts to specific APIs and must not store secrets.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:21.298Z — Agent skills directory
- kind: reference
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_5bd0d1e9ca23d8a5"}

Agent skills are stored in .agents/skills/ and include: changesets, code-review-excellence, copywriting, documentation, documentation-writer, frontend-design, grill-with-docs, mcp-builder, opensource-guide-coach, turborepo-monorepo, vitepress, writing-guidelines. Workspace rules are in .agents/rules/.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:22.273Z — TekMemo release layers
- kind: goal
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_7abd7aec3a5efd85"}

TekMemo release order: 1) Stabilize core protocol and NodeFs storage, 2) AI SDK and AgentFS integration, 3) Recall contracts, embeddings, vector adapters, 4) Reranking contracts and provider rerankers, 5) CLI, MCP, graph memory, benchmark tooling, production hardening. Each layer must be release-ready before moving on.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:23.382Z — TekMemo acceptance gate criteria
- kind: constraint
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_8684b0dab8a2733a"}

TekMemo capability release gate: ESM + CJS builds emitted, type declarations emitted, typecheck passes, unit tests pass, contract tests pass for shared contracts, edge cases covered (path safety, malformed input, provider failure, secret redaction), public docs use @tekbreed/tekmemo root imports, package.json exports expose only root entrypoint, pnpm pack:dry-run contains only publishable files.

## 2026-06-18T08:36:24.709Z — Package boundary rules
- kind: constraint
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_bd699e25fd96fb0e"}

Package boundaries: @tekbreed/tekmemo owns the API surface and protocol contracts. Separate packages exist for CLI (tekmemo-cli), MCP server (tekmemo-mcp-server), provider adapters (tekmemo-adapter-*), benchmark kit (tekmemo-benchmark-kit), and test utilities (tekmemo-testing). The apps/ directory contains docs site and the Cloudflare MCP Worker. Internal tooling uses @repo/ scope.

## 2026-06-18T08:38:56.043Z — Agent TekMemo memory workflow
- kind: decision
- tags: none
- confidence: 1
- source: mcp
- metadata: {"id":"mem_a560c08ee8479752"}

At the start of every task, agents MUST call tekmemo_tekmemo_context with the task description, use tekmemo_tekmemo_recall for additional lookups, adhere to returned memory, and persist new facts via tekmemo_tekmemo_remember. This is enforced via AGENTS.md and ensures TekMemo is the single source of truth for project knowledge across all agent sessions.
